steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: The maximum height gives the vertex $(3,268)$, so the model has the form $f(t)=a(t-3)^2+268$. Use the initial height point $(0,124)$ to find $a$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(t)=-16(t+268)^{2}-3$ — vertex would be $(-268,-3)$, not $(3,268)$. Eliminate.
  • B) $f(t)=-16(t-268)^{2}+3$ — vertex would be $(268,3)$, not $(3,268)$. Eliminate.
  • C) $f(t)=-16(t+3)^{2}-268$ — vertex would be $(-3,-268)$, a minimum below ground. Eliminate.
  • D) $f(t)=-16(t-3)^{2}+268$ — vertex $(3,268)$ and $f(0)=124$. Keep.
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
